summary: All four reviewers independently returned CHANGES_REQUESTED / BLOCKED. The r1 punch list (23 items) closed cleanly — every architecture A1–A10 finding is verified resolved and the two highest-severity r1 archetypes (Identity/Content Conflation, Ambient Key Convention Divergence) are genuinely closed. However, the substantial scope expansion driven by those fixes (plan grew from 4,864 → 8,821 lines, +82%; six new tasks: 7.0, 7.1, 7.2, 7.3, 8, P14-telemetry) introduced 10 new BLOCKING/MAJOR defects. Four convergent findings emerge from multiple reviewers: (CR-1) a cross-plan telemetry counter name collision with Phase 8 production code (`complete_total` vs live `completed_total`); (CR-2) the Task 15 staging runbook omits the Landscape DB delete required by Task 7.0's six-column schema addition; (CR-3) the cache-hit replay path invokes an invented `app.state.landscape` attribute that the same plan elsewhere explicitly states does not exist; (CR-4) defensive `.get()` reintroduced on Tier-2 row data in `_all_rows_succeeded`. The net trajectory is mixed: closed defects stayed closed; but the cycle introduced enough new structural defects that the iterative fix model is showing diminishing returns. Recommend one more targeted fix round focused on the four convergent findings plus the invented-symbol cluster, but the operator should be aware the plan's growth rate (+82% in one round) is a load-bearing signal that fix cycles are no longer net-positive on plan health.

convergent_findings.title: Cross-plan telemetry counter name collision: `complete_total` (Phase 4) vs live `completed_total` (Phase 8 forward-fit)
convergent_findings.evidence_summary: Phase 8 has already shipped two forward-fit OTel counters in production code at `src/elspeth/web/sessions/telemetry.py` lines 309-316 and `src/elspeth/web/composer/telemetry_phase8.py` lines 249, 265: `composer.tutorial.started_total` and `composer.tutorial.completed_total` (with 'd'). Phase 4 Task 8 declares a new counter `composer.tutorial.complete_total` (no 'd') in a new `tutorial_telemetry.py` module. These are different OTel metric names. A dashboard targeting either name will be partially blind. The Cross-Plan Implicit Dependency archetype from r1 — partially resolved at the schema layer — has recurred at the telemetry layer.
convergent_findings.resolution: Phase 4 Task 8 must adopt `composer.tutorial.completed_total` and increment the live `tel.tutorial_completed_total` handle from `sessions/telemetry.py` rather than declaring a duplicate. The forward-fit `record_tutorial_completed()` shim in `telemetry_phase8.py` is replaced by Phase 4's real discrimination logic in the same commit. `skip_total` and `abandon_total` are additive and fine. Add a shared 'Tutorial telemetry contract' section to the overview document listing all canonical counter names so future phases can extend without collision.

convergent_findings.title: Task 15 staging runbook omits the Landscape DB delete required by Task 7.0's schema bump
convergent_findings.evidence_summary: Task 7.0 adds six new columns to `runs_table` and requires a Landscape DB delete on deploy. The Phase 4 overview document's own 'two DB-delete events' table acknowledges this. Task 15's staging smoke runbook (Step 1) deletes only the sessions DB. An operator following the runbook verbatim restarts the service and the SQLite schema validator (or the SQLAlchemy `checkfirst=True` no-op) leaves runs_table without the six new columns. The first cache-hit INSERT then fails with a NOT NULL constraint violation (or silently drops column data depending on dialect). The runbook is self-defeating at the most critical step. This is also asymmetric with Task 1, whose commit body explicitly contains an OPERATOR ACTION footer (quality:R2-M5).
convergent_findings.resolution: Expand Task 15 Step 1 to delete BOTH databases (sessions DB AND Landscape audit DB at `<data_dir>/runs/audit.db`). Add a service-restart verification sub-step (`systemctl status elspeth-web.service`) before proceeding to Step 3. Add an OPERATOR ACTION footer to the Task 7.0 commit message mirroring Task 1's precedent.

convergent_findings.title: Cache-hit replay invokes invented `app.state.landscape` attribute the plan elsewhere states does not exist
convergent_findings.evidence_summary: Task 7's `_replay_cached_content_to_landscape` snippet contains `await request.app.state.landscape.record_synthesised_run(...)`. Task 7.2 Step 1 of the same plan document explicitly states: 'There is no `app.state.landscape.read_run` shortcut and no `app.state.landscape` attribute on the app state.' This is a direct plan-internal contradiction guaranteeing `AttributeError` on the first cache hit. The contradiction was introduced by the r1 fix for A2 (the cross-session attribution leak): the writer added `_replay_cached_content_to_landscape` using an access pattern that exists nowhere in the codebase. Two related invented symbols compound this: (R2-7) `app.state.run_lifecycle_repo` is also asserted to exist by Task 7.2 but does not — no `create_app()` wiring produces it; (R2-9) the `Run` frozen dataclass loaded by `RunLifecycleRepository.get_run()` will not carry the six new Task 7.0 columns unless the dataclass is simultaneously extended, which Task 7.0 does not mention.
convergent_findings.resolution: Decide the actual Landscape write access pattern for FastAPI route handlers (most likely: a dedicated `LandscapeWriteRepository` injected as a FastAPI dependency, or extending session_service with a `record_synthesised_run` method). Apply consistently across Task 7 (write path) and Task 7.2 (read path). Add Task 6 sub-step that wires the chosen repository onto `app.state` (or as a FastAPI Depends factory). Add Task 7.0 sub-step that extends the `Run` dataclass with the six new fields and updates the `run_loader` projection. Add stub test for the replay path.

convergent_findings.title: Defensive `.get()` reintroduced on Tier-2 row data in `_all_rows_succeeded`
convergent_findings.evidence_summary: `_all_rows_succeeded` uses `row.get('rating_error')` and `row.get('rating')` on post-pipeline row data. Per CLAUDE.md, post-source pipeline data is Tier-2: types are trustworthy, field absence is a plugin contract violation and should crash. The current code silently treats a missing `rating` field as 'no rating error' (the inverse of P18's safety intent) — meaning a row missing rating entirely would still pass the cache-write gate, allowing cache poisoning by a partially-errored run. This is precisely the defensive-programming archetype the r1 P15 fix was supposed to eliminate; it slipped back in via the new Task 7 code added during the fix cycle.
convergent_findings.resolution: Replace both `.get()` calls with direct subscript `row['rating_error']` and `row['rating']`. If the row type is a typed dataclass or TypedDict, prefer attribute access. Add comment: 'Direct access per Tier-2 semantics — a KeyError here means an upstream plugin violated its output schema; crash immediately.'

punch_list.what: Adopt `composer.tutorial.completed_total` (with 'd') as the canonical name. Wire Phase 4 emit sites to increment the live counter handle in `sessions/telemetry.py` rather than declaring a duplicate. Delete the forward-fit shim `record_tutorial_completed()` in `telemetry_phase8.py` and replace with real discrimination logic in the same commit. Add a 'Tutorial telemetry contract' section to the overview doc enumerating all canonical counter names.
punch_list.why: CR-1 — reality:R2-1 + systems:R2-S2 convergent; Cross-Plan Implicit Dependency archetype recurrence

punch_list.what: Expand Step 1 of the staging smoke runbook to delete BOTH databases (sessions DB AND `<data_dir>/runs/audit.db`). Add a `systemctl status elspeth-web.service` verification sub-step before Step 3. Add OPERATOR ACTION footer to Task 7.0 commit message body matching Task 1's precedent.
punch_list.why: CR-2 — quality:R2-B1 + systems:R2-S1 convergent; Shifting the Burden worsened from r1

punch_list.what: Resolve the `app.state.landscape` contradiction. Decide concrete Landscape access pattern for FastAPI route handlers (recommend `LandscapeWriteRepository` injected via `Depends`). Apply consistently in `_replay_cached_content_to_landscape` (write) and `audit_story_service` (read). Add Task 6 sub-step wiring the repository. Add Task 7.0 sub-step extending the `Run` frozen dataclass with the six new column fields and updating the `run_loader`. Add stub test for replay path.
punch_list.why: CR-3 — architecture:B-R2-2 + reality:R2-7 + reality:R2-9 convergent on plan-internal contradiction and invented symbols

punch_list.what: Fix `TutorialTurn4Run` to access `response.output.source_data_hash` and `response.output.rows` (nested under output, matching the Task 7.1 TutorialRunResponse schema). Fix the Vitest mock to use the nested shape: `{ run_id, output: { source_data_hash, rows }, seeded_from_cache, cache_key }`. Consider extracting a shared TypeScript types module imported by client.ts AND the component, so future shape drifts cannot pass silently.
punch_list.why: architecture:B-R2-1 — wrong-shape access plus a mock that mirrors the wrong shape means tests pass while real API calls return undefined fields

punch_list.what: Replace `Depends(require_user)` with `Depends(get_current_user)` from `elspeth.web.auth.middleware`. `require_user` does not exist anywhere in `src/elspeth/web/`.
punch_list.why: reality:R2-2 — hallucinated auth dependency; correct symbol is the one used by every other route

punch_list.what: Replace all `/api/users/{user_id}/preferences` references with `/api/composer-preferences` (the authenticated-user-scoped endpoint). Replace `/api/users/{user_id}/sessions` references with the live sessions list endpoint (requires recon). Playwright tests must authenticate as the target user, not name them in the path. There is no `/api/users/{id}` namespace anywhere in the backend.
punch_list.why: reality:R2-3 — 404 at the Playwright level on every assertion

punch_list.what: Supply the required `onDone: (mode: 'guided' | 'freeform') => void` prop at the App.tsx call site for `<HelloWorldTutorial />`. The component interface requires it; the current `return <HelloWorldTutorial />;` is a TypeScript compile error. Update App.tutorial.test.tsx mocks accordingly.
punch_list.why: reality:R2-4 — TS compile failure at mount

punch_list.what: The predicate `if wrote and tutorial_changed and existing.tutorial_completed_at is None` references `existing`, which is not defined in the tutorial-completion code path (only `existing_raw` exists in the live service, and only for `default_composer_mode`). Add an explicit pre-PATCH read of `tutorial_completed_at` analogous to how `existing_raw` captures `default_composer_mode`. Capture as `existing_tutorial_completed_at: datetime | None`. Update the predicate. Add a test asserting double-PATCH-same-value fires the counter exactly once.
punch_list.why: quality:R2-B2 — predicate is undefined at the code level, all five Task 8 discrimination tests are un-implementable

punch_list.what: Drop `run_started_at` (live column is `started_at`). Drop `source_data_hash` and `plugin_versions` from runs_table — `source_data_hash` already exists on `rows_table` (per-row) and `plugin_version` already exists on `nodes_table` (per-node). Run-level aggregates of these create semantic ambiguity (does the run-level hash mean source-config-hash or all-rows-hash?). Keep `seeded_from_cache` and `cache_key` (unambiguous run-level booleans/keys). Map the `RunAuditStoryResponse.run_started_at` field to `landscape_row.started_at` at response-build time.
punch_list.why: reality:R2-5 (run_started_at duplicates started_at) + systems:R2-S4 (denormalization at run level contradicts per-row/per-node records)

punch_list.what: `llm_call_count` does not exist anywhere in `src/elspeth/` (grep returns zero hits). Task 7.0 Step 1 instructs the implementer to find it; the recon will fail. Pick one: (a) expand scope to add the accumulator in engine/orchestrator (additional reviewer cost); (b) defer the column from runs_table; (c) populate from the cache entry only, NULL for non-tutorial runs, document explicitly. Recommend (c).
punch_list.why: systems:R2-S3 — scope materially understated; reconnaissance step is wrong
punch_list.difficulty: low (if option c) or moderate (if option a)

punch_list.what: Replace the type annotation `PipelineState` with `CompositionState` from `elspeth.web.composer.state`. No type named `PipelineState` exists anywhere in `src/elspeth/web/`. Update `_model_id_for_pipeline`'s signature and docstring.
punch_list.why: reality:R2-15 — hallucinated type; will fail at NameError

punch_list.what: Phase 4A defines no backend endpoint for `composePipelineFromPrompt`. Determine: (a) does the tutorial use the existing composer-chat compose loop (wrap that), or (b) is a new single-shot `POST /api/tutorial/compose` endpoint needed? If (b), add it to 21a before 21b1 Task 5 is implemented.
punch_list.why: reality:R2-10 — frontend calls an endpoint the backend plan never defines

punch_list.what: Extract `TutorialRunOutput`, `TutorialRunResponse`, and related models into a dedicated `tutorial_models.py` (or `tutorial_types.py`). Both `tutorial_run_routes.py` and `tutorial_service.py` import from this shared types module. Remove both lazy imports inside function bodies. CLAUDE.md unconditionally forbids lazy imports as the 'Shifting the Burden' archetype.
punch_list.why: architecture:M-R2-1 — two function-body lazy imports introduced to break a circular dep that should be eliminated structurally

punch_list.what: Move Task 7.3 to PR-21b. PR-21a (labeled 'backend') should contain only files under `src/elspeth/*.py` directories and configuration. PR-21b picks up `client.ts` alongside the component implementations that consume it. Restores independent revertability.
punch_list.why: architecture:M-R2-2 — client.ts (TypeScript) in a PR labeled backend undermines the PR-split rationale

punch_list.what: Add `server_default` to the new `nullable=False` columns: `server_default='0'` for `seeded_from_cache`, `server_default='0'` for `llm_call_count`. Without server_default, any existing INSERT path that doesn't yet know about these columns will hit a NOT NULL violation. Audit existing runs_table INSERT sites before landing.
punch_list.why: architecture:N-R2-4 — Python-side `default=False` does not propagate to DDL

punch_list.what: The `elspeth tutorial warm-cache` CLI command is referenced in the runbook but implemented in no task. Add Task 5b (or expand Task 5 Step 6) that ships the Typer subcommand: new 'tutorial' subcommand group, 'warm-cache' subcommand that loads WebSettings, instantiates TutorialCache, calls warm(). Add cache-warm verification to Task 15 Step 3: `ls -1 <data_dir>/tutorial_cache/*.json | wc -l` >= 1 before proceeding to Step 4.
punch_list.why: systems:R2-S6 — runbook references a command no task creates; quality:R2-m1 — no warm verification means cache-warm silent failures go undetected

punch_list.where: 21a Task 8 — telemetry test infrastructure + Task 7.2 — synthesis-forbidden test
punch_list.what: Three test-spec gaps in the new content: (a) Task 8's `tutorial_telemetry.py` counter conftest infrastructure is unspecified — add `tutorial_metric_reader` fixture and `_last_tutorial_counter_attributes` helper mirroring `preferences_metric_reader`; explicitly document the module-import-time rebind contract for the new meter. (b) Parametrize `test_get_audit_story_synthesis_forbidden` over all six required fields, not just `llm_call_count`. (c) Define concrete `@contextmanager` bodies for `_force_one_row_to_error` and `_force_one_row_to_have_null_rating` (P18 integration tests) — name the actual patch target.
punch_list.why: quality:R2-M1 + R2-M2 + R2-M3 — tests un-implementable from plan; safety-critical P18 gate has no concrete fixture

punch_list.what: Replace `row.get('rating_error')` and `row.get('rating')` with direct subscript `row['rating_error']` and `row['rating']` (or attribute access if typed). Comment: 'Tier-2 semantics — KeyError means upstream plugin violated output schema; crash immediately.'
punch_list.why: CR-4 — architecture:M-R2-5 + quality:R2-m2 convergent; defensive `.get()` slipped back in via new Task 7 code

punch_list.where: 21 overview + 21a + 21b1 + 21b2 sibling-plan references; Open Question C3 vs P20
punch_list.what: Three plan-consistency fixes: (a) update sibling-plan references in 21 and 21a to point to the actual 21b1 and 21b2 files (the referenced `21b-phase-4-frontend.md` does not exist). (b) Resolve C3 row vs P20 scope contradiction in the overview — the correct scope is 'wire contract only, UI deferred to sibling settings-panel plan'; remove or correct the C3 row that claims Phase 4 ships the toggle UI. (c) Decide whether the 'sibling settings-panel plan' is a real planned phase with a doc path, or whether Phase 4 should ship a minimal retake link in the existing ComposerPreferencesPanel — see P21.
punch_list.why: architecture:M-R2-3 (stale ref) + architecture:M-R2-4 (internal contradiction)

punch_list.where: 21b1 Task 5 / Task 2 — tutorial state during refresh
punch_list.what: Refresh during Turn 2+ orphans backend sessions unboundedly. Two options: (a) persist `session_id` to sessionStorage keyed by a tutorial nonce so refresh resumes the same session — also fixes the restart-at-turn-1 UX regression; (b) accept orphans and add a background cleanup job for sessions with no associated run record after N hours. Pick (a) — better UX and operationally cleaner. Document the cleanup policy regardless.
punch_list.why: systems:R2-S5 — each refresh after Turn 2 creates one orphaned session; accumulates over user lifetime

punch_list.what: Phase 4 must ship a minimal retake surface in ComposerPreferencesPanel.tsx (e.g., a 'Reset tutorial' link that PATCHes `tutorial_completed_at: null`). Wire contract is already in scope; the UI delta is small. Without this, skip users have no path back to the tutorial until Phase 8 ships, and Phase 4's own risk table acknowledges Phase 8 may slip indefinitely. The 'sibling plan' is not a real planned phase with a doc — it's a hand-wave at the existing component file.
punch_list.why: systems:R2-S7 — Optimistic First-User Path archetype remains active

punch_list.what: Add explicit ordering note: the `RunAuditStoryResponse` Pydantic model must be appended to `schemas.py` BEFORE `audit_story_service.py` is created or the route import is added. Otherwise the service import fails.
punch_list.why: reality:R2-8

punch_list.what: Introduce `TutorialCacheCorruptError(RuntimeError)` named class. Replace three bare `RuntimeError(...)` raises with `TutorialCacheCorruptError(...) from exc`. Register in `app.py`'s global exception handler alongside `CorruptPreferencesError` / `CorruptAuditRowError`.
punch_list.why: reality:R2-11 — CLAUDE.md mandates named exceptions for discriminable handlers

punch_list.what: Update test fixtures to use compound model_id values like `'claude-opus-4-7:claude-3-5-sonnet-20241022'`. Add explicit key-stability test (swapped components produce different keys). Add a test case for model_id with a colon to verify separator unambiguity.
punch_list.why: architecture:N-R2-1 — current tests don't exercise production key format

punch_list.where: 21a Task 6 — TutorialCache.store() cleanup + 21b2 Task 12 — App.tsx bootstrap useEffect
punch_list.what: Two error-swallowing patterns to fix: (a) `except OSError: pass` in temp-cleanup — replace with chained exception or telemetry; (b) `bootstrap().catch((err) => console.error(...))` in App.tsx — replace with writeError dispatch to match the task's own Panel C2 documentation.
punch_list.why: architecture:N-R2-2 + N-R2-3 — silent error swallows contradict CLAUDE.md and the plan's own documented behaviour

punch_list.what: Fixture name in the plan doesn't match the live `in_memory_metric_reader` in `tests/unit/telemetry/conftest.py`. Either import the existing fixture or create a local alias `preferences_metric_reader` in `tests/unit/web/preferences/conftest.py`.
punch_list.why: reality:R2-13

punch_list.what: Add an explicit sub-step: 'Add `tutorial_cache_dir: Path | None = Field(default=None)` to WebSettings in `src/elspeth/web/config.py`.' The field is new (not present in live `WebSettings`). Plan currently cites it as if it exists.
punch_list.why: reality:R2-6

punch_list.what: Two minor telemetry clarifications: (a) document whether `completed_total` counts unique users or raw events (retake-on-the-same-user inflates raw event count); (b) add metric description string for `abandon_total`: 'Lower bound — sendBeacon delivery is best-effort.'
punch_list.why: systems:R2-S8 + R2-S9

punch_list.what: Remove `event.user_term ?? 'this term'` and `event.llm_draft ?? ''` fallbacks in the live component (lines 135-136). With those fallbacks present, the contract test cannot be a genuine gate. Extend the contract test fixture to exhaustively pin every field the component reads.
punch_list.why: quality:R2-M4 — contract test is inert while defensive ?? fallbacks mask field absence

conflicts_resolved.issue: CR-4 severity rating: architecture flagged MAJOR, quality flagged MINOR
conflicts_resolved.resolution: Adopted MAJOR. The cache-write path is safety-critical (cache poisoning would silently serve bad tutorial content). Defensive `.get()` reintroduction also signals the r1 P15 fix didn't take systemically — a process concern beyond the single instance.

conflicts_resolved.issue: Schema duplication (P9) severity: reality flagged MAJOR for run_started_at; systems flagged MAJOR for source_data_hash/plugin_versions — separate findings or one?
conflicts_resolved.resolution: Consolidated into one MAJOR (P9). All three columns represent the same root cause: Task 7.0 column selection wasn't reconciled against the existing rows_table and nodes_table fields. One fix touches all three.

conflicts_resolved.issue: PR-21a contamination (P14): architecture flagged MAJOR; no other reviewer flagged it
conflicts_resolved.resolution: Kept as MAJOR. Single-reviewer findings can be load-bearing when concrete; this one is. Trivial to fix, real consequence if PR-21a is reverted independently.

Shifting_the_Burden.evidence: r1 identified one DB-delete event per schema addition as a debt accumulator. The fix cycle resolved the identity/content problem but added Task 7.0 with six new Landscape DB columns — second DB-delete event in the phase. Task 15 runbook then omits the Landscape DB delete (CR-2), shifting the burden of discovery to the deploying operator at first deploy. The archetype is now expressed as a runbook omission rather than a design pattern.

Identity_Content_Conflation.evidence: TutorialCacheEntry now stores content only; cache-hit replay synthesizes a new run_id owned by the current session. `test_entry_rejects_identity_fields` provides a structural guard via `ConfigDict(extra='forbid')`. No new instances detected in r2.

Cross_Plan_Implicit_Dependency.evidence: Schema-level tutorial_completed_at nullification semantics are now explicitly co-owned with Phase 8 (three-state PATCH documented). However, the archetype resurfaced at the telemetry layer (CR-1): Phase 4 declares `complete_total` while Phase 8 has already committed `completed_total` in production code. Neither plan acknowledges the shared namespace.

Optimistic_First_User_Path.evidence: Warm-cache runbook is now documented. But the warm-cache CLI command itself is implemented in no task (P16) — runbook will fail at first execution. Retake UI is deferred to Phase 8 with no Phase-4 fallback (P21); plan's own risk table acknowledges Phase 8 may slip indefinitely.

Ambient_Key_Convention_Divergence.evidence: `WebSettings.tutorial_cache_dir` now anchored to data_dir convention. No new convention divergences detected in r2.

r1_to_r2_comparison.r1_recurrence_details: P15 (defensive-programming cleanup) was applied to the snippets that existed at r1, but the new Task 7 code added during the fix cycle reintroduced `.get()` on Tier-2 row data in `_all_rows_succeeded` — CR-4/P18.
r1_to_r2_comparison.net_trajectory: MIXED — original defects stayed closed; fix cycle introduced more defects than it closed (10 BLOCKING/MAJOR new vs 6 BLOCKING convergent fixes); the +82% plan growth is a load-bearing signal that fix cycles are passing the diminishing-returns inflection.

revision_effort_estimate: Roughly one full day. The four convergent findings (CR-1 through CR-4) and the invented-symbol cluster (P3, P5, P6, P11) account for the structural risk. CR-3 (P3) is the heaviest single item — it requires a design decision about Landscape write access from FastAPI handlers plus coordinated edits to Task 6 (app wiring), Task 7 (write path), Task 7.0 (Run dataclass extension), and Task 7.2 (read path). CR-1 (P1) requires coordinating Phase 4's plan with Phase 8's already-shipped code, but the fix shape is now spelled out and mechanical. CR-2 (P2) is trivial. P4–P7 are trivial-to-low individually but cluster around the api-contract-drift theme and benefit from being fixed together. The remaining MAJOR findings (P9, P10, P12, P13, P14, P15, P16, P17, P18, P19, P20, P21) are each trivial-to-low individually. The MINOR cluster (P22–P29) is find-and-replace.
next_steps_for_operator: Decide whether to iterate or change strategy (see strategic_recommendation below).

If iterating: dispatch the plan writer with the P1–P8 BLOCKING fixes as the highest priority. P3 needs an explicit design decision from you on the Landscape write access pattern before writer can act.
Pre-clear with Phase 8 author (or yourself, if same) that Phase 4 will adopt `completed_total` and the forward-fit shim will be removed in the same commit (P1).
Run the four reviewer skills + synthesizer once more after the writer cycle; expected verdict is APPROVED_WITH_WARNINGS if all eight BLOCKING items close cleanly and no new defects are introduced.

If a second-round fix cycle introduces more new defects than it closes, the plan should be re-decomposed (split Task 7.0 + 7.2 into a separate phase, or shrink Phase 4 scope back toward the r1 baseline).
ready_for_execution_when: All four convergent findings (CR-1 through CR-4) plus the four BLOCKING invented-symbol items (P5, P6, P7, P8) and the BLOCKING api-contract item (P4) are resolved in the plan files; the eight resolutions are verifiable by grep against the plan documents (counter names, runbook step text, app.state access pattern, response.output access path, require_user/get_current_user, /api/users vs /api/composer-preferences route, App.tsx onDone prop presence, existing_tutorial_completed_at variable defined). Re-review trigger: writer applies the punch list, runs the four reviewer skills + synthesizer. Expected verdict on a clean pass is APPROVED_WITH_WARNINGS (the MINORS P22–P29 are operator-tolerable to land in implementation).

strategic_recommendation.rationale: The four convergent findings are all individually tractable: CR-1 has a spelled-out cross-plan resolution, CR-2 is a runbook-text edit, CR-3 has a clear design pattern (LandscapeWriteRepository via FastAPI Depends), CR-4 is a find-and-replace. The eight BLOCKING punch-list items together are estimated at one focused day of writer work. However, the +82% plan growth and the recurrence of the Cross-Plan Implicit Dependency and Optimistic First-User Path archetypes at new layers are warning signs. The fix cycle is at the diminishing-returns inflection: another full round of speculative content addition would likely introduce more new defects than it closes. Recommend: one tightly-scoped fix round (BLOCKING items only, no new content), then re-review. If r3 surfaces a third generation of new defects from the BLOCKING fixes, the correct response is to shrink Phase 4 scope (defer Task 7.0 + Task 7.2 to a follow-up phase focused exclusively on audit-story Landscape access, leaving Phase 4 with the tutorial UI + cache + telemetry only).
strategic_recommendation.alternative_if_iteration_fails: Scope-shrink Phase 4: defer Task 7.0 (landscape schema) and Task 7.2 (audit-story service) to a dedicated 22-phase-4b-audit-story.md plan. Phase 4 then ships: state machine, cache, telemetry counters, tutorial routes, frontend (the original r1 scope). This eliminates the invented-symbol cluster (which all concentrates in Task 7/7.0/7.2), the schema-duplication concerns, and roughly half the runbook complexity. The audit-story can be a clean greenfield plan once the implementer has seen the actual Landscape read/write surface in the codebase rather than reasoning about it speculatively.
